name: itch.io
description: Open marketplace and publishing platform for indie games. Developer-friendly with flexible pricing, bundle support, HTML5 embedding, and 0-100% revenue share at the devs choice.

seo.title: itch.io — Game Development Tools
seo.meta_description: itch.io: Open marketplace and publishing platform for indie games. Developer-friendly with flexible pricing, bundle support, HTML5 embedding, and 0-100% revenue

when_to_use: Indie game published with pay-what-you-want pricing model
Game jam submission uploaded and playable in browser
HTML5 demo embedded on itch.io page for viral sharing
Early-access beta distributed to community backers for free
Revenue-share set to 100% for developer-owned game sales

pros: HTML5 embedding lets devs host and sell web games
Jam infrastructure is the de facto standard
No gatekeeping, anyone can publish anything
cons: Discovery is weak compared to Steam
Audience skews hobby and jam, not premium buyers
Sales volumes much lower than Steam for same price
Payouts via PayPal and Stripe, no true wallet system
review.verdict: The heart of indie and jam culture, but treat it as marketing and community, not your main revenue channel.
review.best_for: Indies launching prototypes, jam games, and building audience before Steam release
review.not_for: Studios depending on platform discovery and premium-priced releases

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free / hobby tier; no peak-lock evidence.
